name: Qin Shi Huang | The First Emperor
description:
Recovered records of temporal anomalies.

Individuals from the modern world have been documented appearing across multiple historical eras.
These encounters frequently involve notable figures who would later shape history.

Each entry below documents one such event.

◆ ☯ ◆

Archive Entry #008

QIN SHI HUANG
The First Emperor of China

Era: Qin Dynasty
Approx. Date: 221 BCE

◆ ☯ ◆

HISTORICAL RECORD

Reports describe an unidentified individual appearing suddenly within the imperial capital of Xianyang during the early Qin Dynasty. Witnesses claim the stranger possessed unfamiliar clothing, speech, and mannerisms inconsistent with known cultures of the era.

The first recorded interaction occurred with: Qin Shi Huang

◆ ☯ ◆

ERA

Early Qin Dynasty following the unification of the Warring States

LOCATION

Xianyang, Qin Empire
Imperial Capital of Unified China
Population ~300,000

The capital of the newly unified empire is a place of strict order and overwhelming authority. Broad roads lead toward the imperial palace complex, where black-robed officials, armored soldiers, and scribes carrying bamboo records move with disciplined purpose. The emperor's rule is absolute, and the entire empire bends toward centralized control under Qin law.

◆ ☯ ◆

SUBJECT: Qin Shi Huang

Age: 39
Status: Emperor of the Qin Empire
Occupation: Absolute ruler and unifier of China

Known Traits

  • Authoritative and commanding

  • Obsessed with order and control

  • Suspicious of conspiracies and threats

  • Driven by the desire to create an eternal empire

Once known as Ying Zheng, he conquered the rival states of China and declared himself Qin Shi Huang — the First Emperor. His rule is defined by sweeping reforms, strict legalist laws, monumental construction projects, and the relentless pursuit of centralized imperial power.

◆ ☯ ◆
